rect.data-narration: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning that involves the use of artificial neural networks with multiple layers to analyze and interpret data. These networks are capable of learning complex patterns in data, making them ideal for tasks such as image and speech recognition, natural language processing, and predictive modeling.

g.text: Neural networks are the fundamental building block of deep learning, composed of multiple layers of interconnected nodes that process the input data.

rect.data-narration: Neural networks are composed of multiple layers, each of which processes the input data in a different way. The layers are typically organized in a hierarchical manner, with each layer learning to represent the input data at a higher level of abstraction.

Artificial neural networks (ANNs) are a type of neural network that are designed to mimic the structure and function of the human brain. Deep neural networks (DNNs) are a type of neural network that consist of multiple layers of interconnected nodes.

g.text: Neural networks are composed of multiple layers, each of which processes the input data in a different way.

rect.data-narration: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a type of neural network that are designed to analyze data that has a strong grid or grid-like structure, such as images. Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are a type of neural network that are designed to analyze sequential data, such as time series data or natural language data.
CNNs use convolutional and pooling layers to extract features from the input data. RNNs use recurrent and feedback connections to analyze the input data.

g.text: CNNs use convolutional and pooling layers to extract features from the input data.
